applications.description: Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) is an inter Autonomous Systems routing protocol used by most ISPs.
applications.name: APP_BGP

applications.description: The DNS protocol is used to translate internet names (www.site.com) into IP addresses and vice versa.
applications.name: APP_DNS

applications.description: The FTP protocol is used for reliable data transfer between a client and a server.
applications.name: APP_FTP

applications.description: This protocol is used to transport data in data connection of FTP communication.
applications.name: APP_FTP_DATA

applications.description: The Identification Protocol provides a means to determine the identity of a user of a specific TCP connection.
applications.name: APP_IDENT

applications.description: The IMAP protocol (Internet Message Access Protocol Version 4) enables a client to access and manipulate electronic mail messages on a server.
applications.name: APP_IMAP

applications.description: IMAPS is the secure version of the IMAP protocol.
applications.name: APP_IMAPS

applications.description: Internet Printing Protocol (IPP) is a standard used for remote printing using Internet tools and technologies.
applications.name: APP_IPP

applications.description: IPSec protocol provides services for securing hosts communications. IPsec provides two security services: Authentication Header (AH), which allows authentication of the sender and Encapsulating Security Payload (ESP), which allows both authentication of the sender and encryption of data.
applications.name: APP_IPSEC

applications.description: Jabber is an open standard instant messaging and presence system involving the XMPP protocol. [Note: jabber is also known as xmpp.]
applications.name: APP_JABBER

applications.description: The Jetdirect protocol is used by HP network printers.
applications.name: APP_JETDIRECT

applications.description: Kerberos provides a means to verify the identities of the different workstations on an open (unprotected) network.
applications.name: APP_KRB5

applications.description: Layer Two Tunneling Protocol (L2TP) is an extension of the Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol (PPTP) used by an Internet service provider (ISP) to enable the operation of a virtual private network (VPN) over the Internet.
applications.name: APP_L2TP

applications.description: LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) is a protocol used for accessing directory services. Windows environments use this protocol to send queries to Active Directory. [Note: ldap is also known as cldap.]
applications.name: APP_LDAP

applications.description: Secure LDAP is the Secure version of the LDAP protocol.
applications.name: APP_LDAPS

applications.description: NetBIOS defines a software interface and standard methods providing a communication interface between the application program and the attached medium. NetBIOS is used in various LAN (Ethernet, Token Ring etc) as well as WAN environments such TCP/IP, PPP and X.25 networks.
applications.name: APP_NETBIOS

applications.description: The NFS protocol provides transparent remote access to shared file systems across networks as described in RFC 1813.
applications.name: APP_NFS

applications.description: The Network News Transport Protocol (NNTP) is used for the distribution, inquiry, retrieval and posting of net news articles using a reliable stream-based mechanism.
applications.name: APP_NNTP

applications.description: Network Time Protocol (NTP) is a time synchronization system for computer clocks through the Internet network. It provides mechanisms to synchronize time and coordinate time distribution in a large network.
applications.name: APP_NTP

applications.description: Post Office Protocol - Version 3 (POP3) allows a workstation to simply and dynamically access a mail stored on a mail server.
applications.name: APP_POP3

applications.description: Secure version of the POP3 protocol.
applications.name: APP_POP3S

applications.description: Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol allows the Point to Point Protocol (PPP) to be tunnelled through an IP network.
applications.name: APP_PPTP

applications.description: RADIUS (Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service) is a client/server protocol that enables remote access servers to communicate with a central server to authenticate dial-in users and authorize their access to the requested system or service.
applications.name: APP_RADIUS

applications.description: A key component of Terminal Server is the Remote Desktop Protocol which allows a thin client to communicate with the Terminal Server over the network. This protocol is based on International Telecommunications Union's (ITU) T.120 protocol, an international, standard multichannel conferencing protocol currently used in the Microsoft NetMeeting conferencing software product. It is tuned for high-bandwidth enterprise environments and will also support encrypted sessions.
applications.name: APP_RDP

applications.description: RPC (Remote Procedure Call) is an easy and popular paradigm for implementing the client-server model of distributed computing. A request is sent to a remote system to execute a designated procedure, using arguments supplied, and the result is returned to the caller.
applications.name: APP_RPC

applications.description: The Real Time Streaming Protocol (RTSP) is an application-level protocol for control over the delivery of data with real-time properties. RTSP provides an extensible framework to enable controlled, on-demand delivery of real-time data, such as audio and video.
applications.name: APP_RTSP

applications.description: Session Initiation Protocol (SIP) is the Internet Engineering Task Force's (IETF's) standard for multimedia conferencing over IP. Like other VoIP protocols, SIP is designed to address the functions of signaling and session management within a packet telephony network.
applications.name: APP_SIP

applications.description: The Server Message Block Protocol (SMB/SMB2) provides a method for client applications to read and write to files and to request services from server programs in a computers network.
applications.name: APP_SMB

applications.description: The Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP) is a protocol used for transfering mail reliably and efficiently.
applications.name: APP_SMTP

applications.description: SNMP is a request/response protocol that communicates management information between two types of SNMP software entities: SNMP applications (also called SNMP managers) and SNMP agents.
applications.name: APP_SNMP

applications.description: Service Location Protocol is a decentralized, lightweight, scalable and extensible protocol for service discovery within a local area network.
applications.name: APP_SRVLOC

applications.description: Simple Service Discovery Protocol (SSDP) provides a mechanism whereby network clients can discover desired network services.
applications.name: APP_SSDP

applications.description: Secure Shell (SSH), sometimes known as Secure Socket Shell, is a UNIX-based command interface and a protocol for obtaining secure access to a remote computer.
applications.name: APP_SSH

applications.description: Syslog protocol is used for the transmission of event notification messages across networks between a client and a server.
applications.name: APP_SYSLOG

applications.description: Telnet provides a fairly general, bi-directional, eight-bit byte oriented communications facility. Its primary aim is to provide a standard method of interfacing between terminal devices and terminal-oriented processes.
applications.name: APP_TELNET

applications.description: Secure version of the Telnet.
applications.name: APP_TELNETS

applications.description: Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP) is a file transfer protocol that is simpler to use than the File Transfer Protocol (FTP) but that proposes less features. TFTP uses the User Datagram Protocol (UDP) rather than the Transmission Control Protocol (TCP).
applications.name: APP_TFTP

applications.description: This protocol defines a method by which a pager can receive a message over the Internet
applications.name: APP_SNPP

applications.description: Real Time Messaging Protocol (RTMP) is a proprietary protocol developed by Adobe Systems for streaming audio, video and data over the Internet, between a Flash player and a server.
applications.name: APP_RTMP

applications.description: iTunes is an Apple proprietary digital media player application, used for playing and organizing digital music and video files.
applications.name: APP_ITUNES

applications.description: Spotify is an application of musical streaming.
applications.name: APP_SPOTIFY

applications.description: TeamViewer is an application that enables a connection to a remote computer in order to perform maintenance operations. It is also possible to show the current display to a remote computer, to transfer files, and to create a VPN tunnel.
applications.name: APP_TEAMVIEWER

applications.description: NetFlix is a site using Silverlight protocol to stream videos.
applications.name: APP_NETFLIX

applications.description: This protocol plug-in classifies the http traffic to the hosts concur.com and concursolutions.com. It also classifies the ssl traffic to the Common Names concursolutions.com and .concur.com.
applications.name: APP_CONCUR

applications.description: This protocol plug-in classifies the ssl traffic to the Common Name .salesforce.com.
applications.name: APP_SALESFORCE

applications.description: GoToMeeting is an online meeting service developed by Citrix.
applications.name: APP_GOTOMEETING

applications.description: WebEx is an online meeting, videoconferencing and collaborative application
applications.name: APP_WEBEX

applications.description: WINS (Windows Internet Naming Service) is Microsoft's implementation of NetBIOS Name Service (NBNS), which is a name server and service for NetBIOS computer names. This plug-in classifies replication flows between servers. Client-to-Server flows are handled by the nbns plug-in.
applications.name: APP_WINS

applications.description: Citrix GoToMyPC is a secured web-based remote access solution, enabling to take control of a PC/MAC from a web browser.
applications.name: APP_GOTOMYPC

applications.description: The echo service simply sends back to the originating source any data it receives.
applications.name: APP_ECHO

applications.description: TACACS+ (Terminal Access Controller Access-Control System Plus) is a Cisco Systems proprietary protocol which provides access control for routers, network access servers and other networked computing devices via one or more centralized servers.
applications.displayName: Terminal Access Controller Access-Control System Plus
applications.name: APP_TACACS_PLUS

applications.description: Yahoo is a pseudo-protocol which classifies generic web services related to Yahoo.
applications.name: APP_YAHOO

applications.description: Google Hangouts is an instant messaging service, using XMPP, and provides both text and voice communication. [Note: Google Hangouts used to be known as Gtalk.]
applications.displayName: Google Talk (incl. Hangouts and Allo and Duo)
applications.name: APP_GTALK

applications.description: Office 365 is a Microsoft on-line service which gives access to Office applications from the internet.
applications.name: APP_OFFICE365

applications.description: On-line version of the Microsoft Lync IM and VoIP services (included in Office 365).
applications.name: APP_LYNC_ONLINE

applications.description: The iperf protocol is used by the self-titled tool for network performance measures.
applications.name: APP_IPERF

applications.description: Web site and mobile application for testing both bandwidth and latency of any internet connection.
applications.name: APP_SPEEDTEST

applications.description: Zoom Video Conferencing
applications.name: APP_ZOOM

applications.description: ICA Traffic with priority 0
applications.name: APP_ICA_REALTIME

applications.description: Riverbed_
applications.name: APP_RIVERBED

applications.description: RTelnet is a SOCKS client version of telnet in Unix-like systems
applications.name: APP_RTELNET

applications.description: Mitel hosted VoIP
applications.name: APP_MITEL

applications.description: Blue Jeans video
applications.name: APP_BLUEJEANS

applications.description: The H323 Protocol is a VoIP protocol, used for call signaling and RAS (Registration, Admission and Status).
applications.name: APP_H323

applications.description: Line Printer Daemon is for submitting print jobs to a remote printer.
applications.name: APP_LPD

applications.description: Secure File Transfer Protocol is for file access, transfer, and management functionalities over a reliable data stream.
applications.name: APP_SFTP

applications.description: Proprietary control and media streams for the ShoreTel VoIP solution.
applications.name: APP_SHORETEL

applications.description: Protocol used to define communication guidelines between Wireless Termination points and Access Controllers
applications.name: APP_LWAPP

applications.description: AT&T Office@Hand Business Collaboration Service
applications.name: APP_OFFICEATHAND

applications.description: RingCentral cloud unified communications and collaboration
applications.name: APP_RINGCENTRAL
